water.name: Carson River, and all waters in Lahontan Valley

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Carson River, and all waters in Lahontan Valley. Open year around. Daily: 25 game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye. Possession: 50 game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Carson River, Lower — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Below Lahontan Dam, the Carson River flows through the Lahontan Valley toward Fallon and the Carson Sink. Channel catfish and bullheads are caught in the lower reaches, along with largemouth and smallmouth bass.

sources.title: Mercury in Fish — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Carson River from Dayton to the Lahontan Dam and all water in the Lahontan Valley — No consumption of any fish.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.humanSummary: This stretch of the Carson River and all waters in the Lahontan Valley are open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: Of the 25 daily game fish, no more than 5 may be black bass and no more than 5 may be walleye; all other game fish (catfish, bullhead, etc.) count toward the general limit uncapped. In possession (50 fish total), no more than 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye.

regulations.humanSummary: 25 game fish/day (50 in possession), with sub-limits of at most 5 black bass and 5 walleye per day (10 and 10 in possession).
regulations.verbatimText: Limits — Daily: 25 game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye. Possession: 50 game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: State agencies advise against eating any fish caught from the Carson River below Dayton or from any waters in the Lahontan Valley due to methylmercury contamination stemming from historic Comstock-era mining discharges into the Carson River drainage; catch-and-release is recommended.

regulations.humanSummary: Nevada health and wildlife agencies advise against eating any fish caught from the Carson River between Dayton and the Lahontan Dam, or from waters in the Lahontan Valley, due to mercury contamination.
regulations.verbatimText: Carson River from Dayton to the Lahontan Dam and all water in the Lahontan Valley — No consumption of any fish.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
